recipe.title: Incredible naan breads
recipe.description: An easy naan bread recipe that will show you how to make naan bread at home. Amazing as part of a homemade curry feast, or simply topped with a runny egg.

ingredients.name: ½ x 7g sachet of dried yeast

steps.descr: Combine the yeast, honey and 125ml of lukewarm water in...
steps.instructions: Combine the yeast, honey and 125ml of lukewarm water in a bowl, then set aside for 5 minutes, or until starting to bubble.

on_enter.say: Combine the yeast, honey and 125ml of lukewarm water in a bowl, then set aside for 5 minutes, or until starting to bubble.

steps.descr: Melt the ghee in a small pan over a low heat
steps.instructions: Melt the ghee in a small pan over a low heat. Meanwhile, combine the flour and ½ tablespoon of sea salt in a large bowl and make a well in the middle.

on_enter.say: Melt the ghee in a small pan over a low heat. Meanwhile, combine the flour and ½ tablespoon of sea salt in a large bowl and make a well in the middle.

steps.descr: Pour 1 tablespoon of the melted ghee into the well,...
steps.instructions: Pour 1 tablespoon of the melted ghee into the well, followed by the yoghurt and yeast mixture, then beat the wet ingredients together with a fork. Gradually start incorporating the flour from the sides until it starts to form a rough dough, then bring it together with your hands – it should be soft and sticky, so add a splash more water, if you think it needs it.

on_enter.say: Pour 1 tablespoon of the melted ghee into the well, followed by the yoghurt and yeast mixture, then beat the wet ingredients together with a fork. Gradually start incorporating the flour from the sides until it starts to form a rough dough, then bring it together with your hands – it should be soft and sticky, so add a splash more water, if you think it needs it.

steps.descr: Knead the dough on a flour-dusted surface for around 5...
steps.instructions: Knead the dough on a flour-dusted surface for around 5 minutes, or until smooth, then place into a lightly floured bowl.

on_enter.say: Knead the dough on a flour-dusted surface for around 5 minutes, or until smooth, then place into a lightly floured bowl.

steps.descr: Cover with a clean tea towel and leave in a warm place...
steps.instructions: Cover with a clean tea towel and leave in a warm place for 1 hour 30 minutes, or until doubled in size.

on_enter.say: Cover with a clean tea towel and leave in a warm place for 1 hour 30 minutes, or until doubled in size.

steps.descr: Once risen, knock back the dough with your fist, then...
steps.instructions: Once risen, knock back the dough with your fist, then divide into 6 balls. Roll each portion into an oval shape, roughly 1½cm thick.

on_enter.say: Once risen, knock back the dough with your fist, then divide into 6 balls. Roll each portion into an oval shape, roughly 1½cm thick.

steps.descr: Heat a large non-stick frying pan over a high heat, add...
steps.instructions: Heat a large non-stick frying pan over a high heat, add a naan bread and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, or until cooked through, turning halfway.

on_enter.say: Heat a large non-stick frying pan over a high heat, add a naan bread and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, or until cooked through, turning halfway.

steps.descr: Brush with a little of the remaining ghee, sprinkle over...
steps.instructions: Brush with a little of the remaining ghee, sprinkle over a few nigella seeds and a small pinch of salt. Keep warm in the oven while you make the remaining breads. Delicious served with your favourite curry

on_enter.say: Brush with a little of the remaining ghee, sprinkle over a few nigella seeds and a small pinch of salt. Keep warm in the oven while you make the remaining breads. Delicious served with your favourite curry
